displayTitle: The Block Is Not The Part

beats.narration: You standardized perfectly on the block. Then you reach the part — rougher, hotter, coarser. The same flaw echoes back smaller, and the calibration lies.

payload.title: THE BLOCK IS NOT THE PART
payload.sub: Transmission factors — why a perfect setup can still call it wrong.

beats.narration: Don't guess the difference — measure it. Same gain: the block's back echo hits eighty percent, the rough part only forty. That gap is six decibels.

payload.heading: Transfer correction makes it fair
payload.note: MEASURE THE GAP · ADD IT TO GAIN

beats.narration: Three things change between block and part. The transducer wears and drifts. The couplant transfers differently. The material scatters and slows the sound.
Re-standardize whenever the system changes — probe, cable, couplant, settings — and at the close. A dropped response means missed flaws; everything since gets retested.

payload.heading: When the setup must be rebuilt

beats.narration: The calibration is never yours to assume. It is yours to prove — again and again, until the report is signed.
